title: Myasthenic Crisis
chief_complaint: I have myasthenia gravis. I can't swallow anymore and I'm having trouble breathing. I feel like I'm choking on my own saliva. My voice is very weak and nasal.

physical_exam.General: Tachypneic, using accessory muscles. Nasal, staccato speech. Drooling. Unable to handle secretions.
physical_exam.Neurologic: Bilateral ptosis (worse on sustained upgaze). Diplopia on lateral gaze. Weakness of neck flexors (3/5). Proximal weakness in all limbs (4/5). Reflexes normal. Sensation intact.
physical_exam.Respiratory: Single breath count = 8 (normal >30). Vital capacity = 900mL (15 mL/kg). NIF = -18 cmH2O (normal <-60).

hidden_data.abg: pH 7.32, PaCO2 55, PaO2 65, HCO3 28, SaO2 88%. Hypercapnic respiratory failure.
hidden_data.achr_ab: AChR antibodies strongly positive (15 nmol/L, normal <0.5)

correct_diagnosis: Myasthenic Crisis with Hypercapnic Respiratory Failure
key_learning_points: Myasthenic crisis = respiratory failure from MG requiring intubation or non-invasive ventilation. 20% of MG patients experience crisis in their lifetime
Precipitants: Infection (40%), medication change, surgery, pregnancy, aspiration, medication non-adherence
Warning signs: Single breath count <15, VC <15 mL/kg or <1L, NIF <20 cmH2O (less negative = weaker), inability to handle secretions, staccato speech
Treatment: PLEX (5 exchanges) or IVIG (0.4g/kg ×5 days). Both equally effective. PLEX faster onset (days), IVIG easier to administer
AVOID: High-dose steroids acutely (paradoxical worsening). Start low, go slow. AVOID: Aminoglycosides, Fluoroquinolones, Beta-blockers, Magnesium (all worsen MG)
Intubation: If VC <15 mL/kg, NIF <20, or rising PaCO2. Use Rocuronium cautiously. AVOID Succinylcholine (unpredictable response)
